findings.title: Missing transaction rollback on partial user creation failure
findings.description: The createUser service method writes to two tables sequentially without a transaction. If the second write fails, the first write is not rolled back, leaving the database in an inconsistent state.

findings.title: Email validation does not check format, only presence
findings.description: The userValidator checks that the email field is present but does not validate it against an RFC 5321 pattern, allowing malformed addresses through.

findings.title: N+1 query pattern in user repository list method
findings.description: The listUsers method fetches each user's roles with an individual query per user rather than a single JOIN, causing N+1 query behaviour under load.

findings.title: No rate limiting on user registration endpoint
findings.description: The registerUser controller endpoint does not apply rate limiting, enabling automated account-creation abuse and credential stuffing attacks.

findings.title: Missing transaction rollback on partial user creation failure
findings.description: The createUser method lacks atomicity; a failure after the first DB write leaves orphaned records.

findings.title: User model email field lacks a database index
findings.description: Queries that filter or sort by email will perform full table scans as the email column has no index defined in the model schema.

findings.title: No rate limiting on registration endpoint
findings.description: The registration route has no rate-limit middleware, making it trivial to enumerate accounts or flood the registration flow.

findings.title: N+1 query in user list retrieval
findings.description: Each user record triggers a separate roles lookup; consolidating with a JOIN or a bulk fetch would reduce DB round trips significantly.

path_and_reason_header: Review path: multi-model (above-threshold diff; reviewers: 2 native + 2 external)
